title: Stelis pseudocheila
taxonomy: Stelis pseudocheila belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This species is classified within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which includes many small, delicate orchids. The genus Stelis contains over 1,000 species, making it one of the largest and most diverse genera within the orchid family. Stelis pseudocheila is native to Central and South America, where it grows in a variety of habitats, including cloud forests and montane regions. The classification of Stelis pseudocheila is also supported by its characteristic flower shape and size.

intro: Stelis pseudocheila is a species of orchid, a type of flowering plant characterized by its unique and often exotic appearance. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections.
